title: Ensure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters have the  monitoring service enabled

input_row.title: Ensure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters have the  monitoring service enabled
input_row.description: Metrics help you monitor the health and performance of your clusters. Create a monitoring configuration to forward cluster and app metrics to IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig. Use the IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service observability plug-in to create a monitoring configuration for IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig in your cluster, and use this monitoring configuration to automatically collect and forward metrics to IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig. With IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig, you can collect cluster and pod metrics, such as the CPU and memory usage of your worker nodes, incoming and outgoing HTTP traffic for your pods, and data about several infrastructure components. In addition, the agent can collect custom application metrics by using either a Prometheus-compatible scraper or a StatsD facade. IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig is a cloud-native, and container-intelligence management system that you can include as part of your IBM Cloud architecture. Use it to gain operational visibility into the performance and health of your applications, services, and platforms. It offers administrators, DevOps teams and developers full stack telemetry with advanced features to monitor and troubleshoot, define alerts, and design custom dashboards. IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig is operated by Sysdig in partnership with IBM.
input_row.rationale: Gain operational visibility into the performance and health of your apps and your cluster by deploying a Sysdig agent to your worker nodes. The agent collects pod and cluster metrics, and sends these metrics to IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig. Get critical Kubernetes and container insights for dynamic microservice monitoring. IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig auto-discovers Kubernetes environments providing out-of-the-box dashboards and alerts for clusters, nodes, namespaces, services, deployments, pods and more. A single agent per node dynamically discovers all microservices and auto-collects metrics and events from various sources including Kubernetes, hosts, networks, containers, processes, applications and custom metrics like Prometheus, JMX, and StatsD. Mitigate the impact of abnormal situations with proactive notifications. IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig includes alerts and multi-channel notifications that you can use to reduce the impact on your day to day operations and accelerate your reaction and response time to anomalies, downtime, and performance degradation. Notification channels that you can easily configure include email, slack, PagerDuty, Webhooks, OpsGenie, and VictorOps.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Click Menu icon --> Resource list 3. Select your cluster under Clusters to go to the details page. 4. Select the Overview tab 5. Under Monitoring there should be a button to Launch the Sysdig dashboard. If instead the button is labeled Connect, then monitoring is not enabled. From Command Line: 1. Check whether a logging configuration is added to your cluster. If one is added, the output of this command will include the monitoring instance name and ID along with some other information. ibmcloud ob monitoring config list --cluster
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Click Menu icon --> Resource list 3. Select your cluster under Clusters to go to the details page. 4. Select the Overview tab 5. Under Monitoring click Connect. 6. Select the region and the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig service instance that you want to use (or create an instance), and click Connect. From Command Line: 1. Create the Sysdig monitoring configuration. ibmcloud ob monitoring config create --cluster -- instance
input_row.impact: Before you provision an instance, consider the following information: • The account owner can create, view, and delete an instance of a service in the IBM Cloud. This user can also grant permissions to other users to work with the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig service. • Other IBM Cloud users with administrator or editor permissions can manage the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig service in the IBM Cloud. These users must also have platform permissions to create resources within the context of the resource group where they plan to provision the instance. After you provision an instance, you must configure metric sources, enable platform metrics, or both. After the IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig agent is deployed in a metric source, collection and forwarding of metrics to the instance is automatic. The IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig agent automatically collects and reports on pre-defined metrics. You can configure which metrics to monitor in an environment. You can only configure 1 Sysdig instance in a region to automatically collect platform metrics. Standard pricing for IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig applies.
input_row.default_value: IBM Cloud Monitoring with Sysdig is not configured by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field provides specific commands to use with the IBM Cloud CLI, which shows that this control can be verified without manual intervention.
- The control details the usage of the `ibmcloud ob monitoring config list --cluster` command to verify if the monitoring configuration is present.
- The task relates to a standard IBM Cloud service configuration, which can be monitored through API calls and CLI commands.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_cluster_monitoring_service_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use the IBM Cloud CLI command:
   ```
   ibmcloud ob monitoring config list --cluster <cluster_name>
   ```
   This command checks whether a monitoring configuration for the specified Kubernetes cluster exists.

2. Enumerate Kubernetes cluster resources within the specified account and region to identify which clusters require monitoring verification.

3. The automated check should validate that:
   - A configuration entry exists for the monitoring service in the output of the command.
   - Look for the presence of monitoring instance name and ID in the output.
   
4. **Pass/Fail criteria:**
   - **Pass:** If the output includes a monitoring instance name and ID for the specified Kubernetes cluster.
   - **Fail:** If the output does not show a monitoring configuration or is labeled "Connect" instead of "Launch".

This approach ensures that the monitoring requirements are being met programmatically and allows for quick audit assessments across multiple clusters.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The use of IBM Cloud CLI commands, specifically `ibmcloud ob monitoring config list --cluster <cluster_name>`, allows for programmatic verification of the monitoring configuration without manual intervention. This aligns with the capabilities of IBM Cloud APIs and CLI, which are designed to automate such checks efficiently.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are technically accurate. The command `ibmcloud ob monitoring config list --cluster <cluster_name>` is valid for checking the presence of a monitoring configuration in IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service clusters.
- The criteria for Pass/Fail are correctly defined based on the presence of a monitoring instance name and ID.
- The program name `ibm_kubernetes_cluster_monitoring_service_enabled` is descriptive and follows a logical naming convention, although it could be slightly more detailed to specify the type of monitoring (e.g., `sysdig`).

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming to `ibm_kubernetes_cluster_sysdig_monitoring_enabled` to specify the type of monitoring service explicitly.
- **Missing Considerations:** The review could include additional checks for the configuration's status (e.g., ensuring it is active and not just present). This could involve verifying the operational status of the Sysdig monitoring service.
- **Alternative Methods:** While the CLI is sufficient, exploring IBM Cloud SDKs for integration into broader automation frameworks could enhance flexibility and integration capabilities.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded with a clear understanding of IBM Cloud CLI capabilities. The automation approach is appropriate for the control in question, and the technical details provided are robust.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Modify the program name to `ibm_kubernetes_cluster_sysdig_monitoring_enabled` for clarity and specificity.
- Consider adding checks for the operational status of the monitoring service to ensure it is not only configured but also active and functioning as expected.
